title: pilosella auriculoides subsp. echiocephala
intro: Pilosella auriculoides subsp. echiocephala, a lesser-known member of the dandelion family (Asteraceae), is a perennial plant native to Europe, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pilosella auriculoides subsp. echiocephala include its potential to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or making teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
